name: Hockey Stick
preceding_figure_names: Advanced Hip Twist, Closed Hip Twist, Cross Basic, Curl, Fan, Natural Top, Open Hip Twist, Opening Out From Reverse Top, Spiral, Sweetheart
following_figure_names: Open Basic Movement, Shoulder To Shoulder Left Side, Time Step LF, Alemana, Open Hip Twist, Curl, Chase, Turkish Towel, Sweetheart, Follow My Leader, Three Cha Cha Chas Forward, New York LSP, Spot or Switch Turn To R, Underarm Turn to L, Cuban Break LF, Split Cuban Break LF
notes:
Other Finishing positions:
Open CPP
Man leads Lady to overturn towards end of 7, both taking 8 diagonally fwd in Open CPP to chasse.
Lady turns an additional 1/4 L on RF using a loosely crossed spiral action.
Left Side Position (LSP)
Man leads Lady to overturn towards end of 7, then turns 1/8 R to chasse fwd, lowering L arm and taking it fwd to below shoulder level.
Lady turns towards end of 7 as for CPP above, then turns an additional 1/8 L, to chasse fwd
Forward Runs could be used in place of last chasse

steps.action: Step forward
Transfer weight
Compact Chasse LRL
comments: The hand goes up on 1, but remains to the right side of the lady.

steps.action: Cuban Cross behind LF, turn 1/8 to R
comments: Hand is brought across the lady's face, causing her to turn 1/8. On the & after 3, the lady is turned a half-turn.

steps.action: Small step forward
Forward Lock in Open Position RLR
comments: Keep going straight, in front of Man, don't veer off to left.
Small steps, don't go too far, create arm window, R hand on left shoulder, make sure both shoulders down, don't raise shoulders.
Difference from Alemana: R hand directly comes up and then place it on Lady's left shoulder.

Alternative - do checked chasse instead of forward lock: 4: RF fwd; &: check: make sure it has 50% weight, do not put all weight on front foot; 1: RF closes to LF, twist hip; 2: LF fwd | 1/8 to L; Step straight first, then turn 1/8 to L on this step, so next step is diagonal fwd (making it a hockey stick). Man's arm should be in front of Lady's face, or already over on Lady's L shoulder. (Lady can help putting his arm on her L shoulder.)
3: RF fwd in line with LF, then turn underarm to end RF back | 1/2 to L, body turns less; Forward Walk Turn technique.
End of beat \"3&\": Do flexible turn - turn upper body first (leaving head and foot where it is), then sharp turn head and hips.
After turning, weight is more forward (chest forward, not leaning body forward which is too much, suck tummy). Don't be back weighted. Use front LF to push the floor and release to step back.

4 & 1: Back Lock in Open Position LRL | body completes turn; Spine moves straight back, do not veer off to left side; Give the Man tone in the arm; Man/Lady together is not on same line as started, turned 1/8, that's why it's a hockey stick.

comments: Use CBM and rise a bit early to lead heel turn.

steps.action: RF to side
comments: Get strong rotation through the feet, do not come off RF too early, which will allow Lady to turn.

steps.action: LF to side and slightly forward
comments: Body turns less
Sway(S)

steps.action: RF back
comments: Keep your own balance.
When starting the figure, feel left hip flexor working, do not think about R leg moving back, think about standing on the L leg to get weight moving into the foot.

steps.action: LF closes to RF (Heel Turn)
comments: Heel turn, the feeling is the turn is mostly done on RF, has a slight feeling of pushing off LF, then RF step back on 3 to finish.
Stay connected with Man.

steps.action: RF to side and slightly back

name: Closed Wing
preceding_figure_names: Basic Weave, Chasse From Promenade Position, Closed Telemark, Outside Change, Turning Lock, Weave From Promenade Position
following_figure_names: Closed Telemark (taking step 1 in CBMP OP on L side), Open Telemark (taking step 1 in CBMP OP on L side), Double Reverse Spin (taking step 1 in CBMP OP on L side), Progressive Chasse To R (taking step 1 in CBMP OP on L side), Reverse Turn (taking step 1 in CBMP OP on L side), Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot, Whisk (if ended DW)
notes: Keep upward balanced poised position, don't try to pull hip back to get around. This step is not too big. Keep it underneath body. (Man is not moving anywhere) Lady keeps her head upward and leftward on step 3 and keep her poise back. L leg goes ahead of body to Wing position. Try not to think body rotating. Think about sway. Try to keep shoulders parallel to partner.

starts to close to RF
comments: Slight body turn to L on 2 and 3
Slight rise on 2 and 3 NFR

steps.action: closes to RF w/o weight
back in CBMP

steps.action: to side and slightly back (small step)
comments: Slight sway to L on 2 and 3
continue to rise on 2

steps.action: fwd in CBMP, OP on L side
comments: Slight body turn to L
Up. Lower e/o 3

name: Contra Check
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin, Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot, Hesitation Change, Reverse Pivot, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Chasse From Promenade Position, Weave From Promenade Position, Cross Hesitation, Wing, Left Whisk
notes: Focus: smoothness, rotation, elasticity, lightness, softness, finish the extension.

steps.action: Step forward in CBMP, pointing almost LOD
Transfer weight back to RF, turning body to R
comments: If preferred, could be LF fwd with foot flat.

steps.action: Step to side in Promenade Position, moving along LOD
Step back in CBMP, feet almost BLOD, turning body to L
comments: The step needs to feel more free. Lady does not bend straight back, there is rotation and head continues to extend go along body line.

Prep: bend/lower left knee, shoulder level off, rotate hips to left. Do not send stomach muscle forward, i.e. do not stick tummy up to Man at this time. connect center into hip joint, hip has to be able to move.
Big step back after rotating hips (if step is too short, upper body weight will take over when doing contra check), also need bigger step to create more extension. CMBP (do not get into Man's space).

RF point back first, RF toe turned in (for better balance), then on ball of feet, keep right heel up (solid, not heel moving down and then up), weight split. body rotate to left, but keep left side up to Man (though body is turning left and shaping). Don't break neck.
Keep left toe down on floor, do not lift L toe. LF is pretty much flat on floor.

Flex both knees, at this time, weight in the middle, not on back foot. Get there first, then give (R) hip and ribs to Man for extension, left leg can go more straight at this time, allowing for more extension, putting a bit more weight on RF, take the center back, but keep R heel up, R leg is bent, then extend upper body diagonally back. Extend R arm to Man.
Lift glute and center up to Man. (butt cheek up) and give whole R side to Man. Imagine stretching R side longer to give to Man more, effect is shaping and extending to left.

Upper body has the look of rotating/shaping to left (but don't rotate away from Man, upper body and hip to Man), head "finishes" the extension with breathing. (Don't be afraid to extend head more. Go into Man's R hand.)

The whole body is not one straight piece. There is torch in upper body - rotation and sway (do not just bend backward) and at tail end, stretch even more before recovering. The elasticity doesn't stop.
Continuous extension and all the way to head. Upper body, not locked and still, but stay soft and flexible, go and fill the space Man created with his R hand.
Don't "sit", don't just go "down" which feels heavy. Continuous shaping and then bounce up. Man start recovering after Lady finish extending head.
Arms belong to partner, never pull arms back. extend the arm to give space.
Frame: breathe, need to feel "free". Create elasticity feeling, continuous movement out and come back (no stopping and then rotate. movement is smooth and light.)

steps.action: Transfer weight forward to LF, facing DC against LOD, turning body to R
comments: Make sure to stay very left (has tendency to shift to R, get into Man's space). Keep big frame and give R side to Man, don't fall to PP.
At end, start articulating head turn

steps.action: Step to side in PP, moving LOD, turning 1/4 to R between 2-3
comments: Articulate head.
Make sure R side is to Man.
Prep for next step: Lower first and step big.

name: Double Reverse Spin
preceding_figure_names: Closed Change RF, Closed Wing, Double Reverse Spin, Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot, Hesitation Change, Reverse Pivot, Reverse Turn, Wing
following_figure_names: Drag Hesitation, Closed Change, Whisk, Double Reverse Spin, Reverse Turn, Closed Telemark, Open Telemark, Progressive Chasse
notes:
Advanced dancers often use the timing of 123& when the beat value would be 1, 1, 1/2, 1/2.
Per coach: Timing: do 1 2 &3. Man's early rise signals Lady should do heel turn. Lady does RF "back", L heel closes to R heel, turn, rise and "forward", then RF "out" to side (after turning) and "cross", stay on left.
Note: The Double Reverse Spin may be started DC, DW, or LOD. The amount of turn may vary between 3/4 and a whole turn.
3/4 turn:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/2 between 2-3, or 3/8 between 1-2, 3/8 between 2-3
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2, 1/4 between 2-3, 1/8 between 3-4
7/8 turn:
as shown in chart
whole turn:
Man: 3/8 between 1-2, and 5/8 between 2-3.
Lady: 1/2 between 1-2, 3/8 between 2-3, 1/8 between 3-4.

steps.action: LF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | Starts to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise at end of 1
comments: Important to do CBM. Give Lady the indication of left by lowering.
Early rise is to lead Lady into closing her feet into a heel turn.

steps.action: RF to side | BDW | T | 3/8 btwn 1-2 | Sway(S) | Up
comments: Wait until the lady's feet are together before taking this step.
Push LF to take the RF step.
When taking step, point RF forward with straight knees, then step onto the straight knee and turn to L.
Track your RF, don't do ronde. It will help with balance.
Think about getting hips around, rather than shoulders or feet. Same for second step on any turn where Man is going forward.
Lengthen spine, don't bend forward.

steps.action: LF closes to RF without weight (Toe Pivot) | Facing DW | T | 1/2 btwn 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up, Lower at end of 3
comments: settle and then drive to next step. Keep lengthening the spine.

steps.action: RF to side and slightly back | Backing wall | T | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up
comments: Be active, Lady "takes over", swing forward. Bigger step, push off left foot, get around (or "through") partner with more power.
RF forward should be crossing LOD, Man is at one place, do not peel off from him, keep hips distance (on the right side), don't gap.
Stay with Man, keep on his R side.

steps.action: RF back | BLOD | TH | starts to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
comments: Very important to do CBM, with CBM shoulder will clear way for Man to pass. Keep shoulder flat, and do not drop it on one side, do not pull left shoulder away from Man (It will "feel" left shoulder is "up", but in fact it will be flat.)
Lift center, shoulder slightly to R, left side positive on left turning figures.
Step directly back moving down the floor, turn RF in. Do not step to the side, bigger step.
Start the turn with head (not including shoulder), using head weight. Keep the head going left and look over your shoulder the whole time to avoid being pulled into the center of the turn.
Stay very left to Man, give left side to Man more, do not get in front of Man.
Do not pull left shoulder away from Man, i.e. do not peel off left side. Head can stretch more to left, move center (suck tummy). right arm to Man (this is from extending back).
Use Man, lower first, move center, and then stepping RF/ R leg back, get on to the leg. Don't fall back or lean forward. Keep spine upright.
Show off extension during the whole spin figure by looking over the shoulder more.

steps.action: LF closes to RF (Heel turn) | Facing LOD | HT | 1/2 between 1 and 2 | Sway(S) | Cont to rise on 2
comments: Don't lift toes off ground (no need to show off the bottom of your shoe!), be sure feet are parallel and together.
Draw trailing foot in, close the foot slowly, both legs should straighten, do not rise too early (though Man is rising), rise after heel turned, weight transferred to LF.

Use the rules of head movement, head turns first, slightly more to left (don't do it too early, though). Keep head left to follow left elbow, and look over left shoulder the whole time to avoid being pulled into the center of the turn.
Keep body contact with Man throughout the heel turn.
Do not allow spine to straighten up, keep left.
Do not pause the turning action between 1 and 2, shoulder and head continue to turn left (while feet are together), then body follows in step 3 on '&'.
Movement is continuous, but speed is different. "&3" is faster.

steps.action: RF forward should be crossing LOD, Man is at one place, do not peel off from him, keep hips distance (on the right side), don't gap.
comments: Be active, Lady "takes over", swing forward. Bigger step, push off left foot, get around (or "through") partner with more power.
RF forward should be crossing LOD, Man is at one place, do not peel off from him, keep hips distance (on the right side), don't gap.
Stay with Man, keep on his R side.

steps.action: LF crosses in front of RF | Backing DW | TH | 1/4 between 3-4 | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 4
comments: Cross feet (LF maintains pressure on ground while dragging to cross RF), lower into plie. Hips up the Man.
Do not lower too early, lower on "&"
Note: The Double Reverse Spin may be started DC, DW, or LOD. The amount of turn may vary between 3/4 and a whole turn.

name: Hover Corté
preceding_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Natural Spin Turn, Outside Spin, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Back Lock, Outside Change, Fallaway Whisk, Back Whisk, Outside Spin
notes: Options: keep head closed or open to PP (especially when dancing extra bar of music). Big step. Swing forward. Whole R side to Man, stretch. To create big shape, breathe the frame. After stepping RF forward, turning body from bottom to up, feet, hip, upper toros, and lastly head (articulate head). Really delay head, slow. Feels like head isn't finishing till way into next step. Then articulate LF coming out. Do not shape from lower waist area. Think about high jump, "up and over", pick up center. When developing the shape, do not take arms back, leave arms to Man, extend. Count 3 4 5: RF moves towards LF | Towards alignment of 6 | T LF and pressure on IE T RF | Continue to turn body on step 2 | Sway(R R R) | -. Articulate LF coming out. soft knees, prepare next step by controlled lowering of knees. Count 1: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC against LOD | H | - | Sway(S) | CBM | -.

Fwd, facing LOD, start to turn left, sway S, CBM, start to rise
comments: Big step.

steps.action: Side and LF brushes towards RF, facing center to end facing DC against LOD, T, 1/4 btwn 1-2; continue to turn 1/4 on 2, sway L
comments: Swing forward. Whole R side to Man, stretch, To create big shape, breathe the frame. After stepping RF forward, turning body from bottom to up, feet, hip, upper toros, and lastly head (articulate head). Really delay head, slow. Feels like head isn't finishing till way into next step.

steps.action: LF diag. fwd, facing DC against LOD, TH, - , sway S, - , up lower e/o 6

name: Reverse Corte
preceding_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Natural Spin Turn, Outside Spin, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Natural Turn (4-6; or 4-6 when ending BDW), Fallaway Whisk, Back Whisk (normal; or Back whisk with turn at corner), Closed Impetus (when ending BDW), Back Lock (when ending BDW), Outside Change (at corner; or into Closed Wing), Open Impetus (when ending BDW), Outside Spin (underturned)
notes:
Note:
On step 2 turn may be made on ball of RF with foot flat. Footwork would then be 1. THT; 2 Toes both feet; 3 TH(RF)
Note: Other alignments (between steps 1-2) are the following, with different ending alignment:
backing LOD, 1/2 L between 1-2, ending against LOD
backing LOD, 5/8 L between 1-2, ending BDW against LOD
backing DC, 1/4 to L between 1-2, ending BDC against LOD.
Follower
Count 1: LF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 1
This is the same footwork as second half of Reverse Turn.
Use CBM to help with the turn and stay together with Man.
Do not drive using upper body. Connect shoulder with hip joint, release hip joint forward which starts the motion.
Count 2: RF to side | Facing Center | T | 1/4 turn between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Continue to rise on 2 and 3
Track RF.
Do not step too big, it will pull man off his feet.
Stay very left. Connect L shoulder down. If spine travels to the right, it will pull weight over to right and off balance.
Show off extension between 2 and 3.
Count 3: LF closes to RF | DC against LOD | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | lower e/o 3
Gracefully collect and get back (especially when extensions last extra measure.)
Step 1 of all following figures would be CBMP, Lady OP, RF fwd.
Note: When Man uses different alignments as above, the Lady will turn:
Just over 1/4 to L between 1-2, just under 1/4 to L between 2-3
3/8 to L between 1-2, 1/8 to L between 2-3
1/8 to L between 1-2, 1/8 to L between 2-3

comments: Do not step sideways, straight back, use CBM

steps.action: LF closes to RF without weight, BDC against LOD (or new LOD), heel then toes both feet, sway R, rise on 2
comments: Track foot. Drag LF in with heel. No rondes. Keep feet flat, keep knees flexed. Do not pop up both toes too early. Stay down till lady gets on her RF, then rise.

steps.action: Position held, BDC against LOD, sway R, rise on 3, lower down through right foot

name: Reverse Pivot
preceding_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Natural Spin Turn, Outside Spin, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Closed Change LF (when ending DW), Whisk (when ending DW), Double Reverse Spin (when ending DW; or when ending LOD; or when ending DC), Contra Check (when ending DW), Reverse Turn (1-3 into Basic Weave when ending LOD; or when ending DC), Progressive Chasse To R (3/8 turn, when ending LOD; or when ending DC), Closed Telemark (when ending DC), Open Telemark (when ending DC), Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot (when ending DC)
notes: If the Reverse Pivot is started backing DC the alignment for the Pivot (Man) would be "to center, toe turned in". Note also that this step is in CBMP owing to the body turning strongly to L as the RF foot slips back. This step is on the half beat, at end of last step of previous figure (e.g. Closed Impetus) - body has not completely lowered in previous figure when stepping on this step. Allow this step to blend smoothly with the preceding step, if necessary borrowing from the next beat.

Keep head weight on your left.

LF fwd in CBMP (small step) RF held in CBMP | DC | TH | up to 1/2 L (Pivot) |
Every time when turning to the left, imagine the belly button and center slightly turning to the right.
Do not allow shoulder to completely line up with hips.
R shoulder and R hip feel slightly open in left turning figures.
Step forward, head goes more left. Show off extension.
Controlled lowering at the end, before next step.

name: Weave from PP (after Open Impetus Turn)
preceding_figure_names: Back Whisk, Contra Check, Fallaway Whisk, Open Impetus, Open Telemark, Outside Change, Turning Lock, Turning Lock to R, Whisk
following_figure_names: Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn, Wing (when ended in PP), Left Whisk (when ended in PP), Chasse From Promenade Position (when ended in PP), Closed Wing, Cross Hesitation (when ended in PP)
notes: Man: 1/8 to L can be made between 2-3, so 4-5 may be taken BLOD. Less turn can be made on Whisk to end facing LOD, or the Open Impetus could be used an an entry, when the body would be facing LOD. Then, the alignment will be like the Weave in Foxtrot. This may end in PP, same as Outside change to PP from step 4.

steps.action: RF fwd and across in PP and CBMP, Pointing DC, body facing LOD, traveling DC
comments: Start to rise at end of 1

3 different points in PP: base waist area down to feet, rotate slightly to left; belly button low center, slightly different angle; sternum high center. 3 different points enables you to stay parallel to Lady. Do not turn hips and feet too much to the Lady (makes position awkward). For this left turning figure, keep the differential consistent while moving in a curve, maintain upper body toward to Lady. Don't let upper body rotate further than feet, which will pull the lady to your left.

steps.action: LF fwd, Facing center, T, cont to turn L, CBM, Cont to rise on 2 and 3
comments: Continue to rise on 2 and 3
Rotate the hip, open the door for Lady. Stay parallel.

steps.action: RF to side and slightly back, BLOD, TH, 1/4 btwn 2-3, Up. Lower at end of 3
comments: Do not anticipate moving LOD. Allow energy to continue to move / follow through diagonal center.
Lead Lady outside partner. Use CBM. Lower through right leg and hip, use abdominal muscle, dance diagonal stretch.

steps.action: LF fwd in PP and CBMP, Facing DC, traveling DC, start to turn L, CBM, start to rise e/o 1
comments: Both step 1 and 2, think about stepping straight forward, forward, do not curve into Man, do not turn too early (don't turn late either). Legs are ahead of body. Big step. Stay connected with Man. Work the R side a lot to stay with Man.
Shaping: starting from PP (L hip up). on count 1, R hip begins to pick up.

steps.action: RF to side and slightly back, BDC, T, 1/4 to half turn btwn 1-2, Cont to rise on 2 and 3
comments: Swing leg strongly forward, big step. Don't turn right away. Head traveling forward with the RF first, Get weight on RF, balance on RF, then turn body and head. Don't lose R side. Give R side and hip to Man. Do not just rise and let body fall backward. Think about going forward along the curve which will give you better rotation, give Man more space.
At end of this step, R hip is swung up at its maximum. Think about "swing up and hover".
Always stay on left side (Man's right). Give R side to Man. Stretch, create bigger, fuller shape.

steps.action: LF to side and slightly fwd, Pointing DC, TH, 1/2 btwn 2-3, body turn less, Up. Lower e/o 3
comments: It may feel like a diagonally backward step, but because of the turn it will end side and slightly fwd. Do not do directly side step, that will not leave enough room for CBMP in next step.
Count 1: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDC | HT } -- | -- | Cont to rise e/o 4

To go outside forward, relax R shoulder down, do not take it back (which peels off from Man), connecting R shoulder down will drive the R leg forward. Think of Man's elbow as limbo bar, try not to beat it with upper body forward.
Contra body. Left side more forward. Stretch head even more.
Count 2: LF fwd | FDC | T | start to turn L | -- | CBM | Rise e/o 5

LF to left of Man, stay very left/behind Man. Get on LF first, then: If the figure finishes in PP: turn to PP, don't fallback off to PP. Turn head when rising (after getting weight on foot), feet turn to PP as well.
Pull center back whenever you rise, be on your own balance.
Count 3: RF to side and slightly back | BDW | TH | 1/4 btwn 5-6, body turns less | -- | Up. Lower e/o 6
This is closed finish. keep head position and only turn body, give R side to Man, then head ends up to R and body shapes to R.
It could end in PP: lift left hip to Man, think about high jump.
R side to Man. Check R arm/elbow.
